description: Key rotation verification scenarios with cmn.json history entries and old-key rotation proofs.

cases.description: Content signed with the current active key verifies successfully
Content signed with a retired key verifies when history includes an old-key rotation proof
cases.note: Verification checks current key first, then falls back to history entries with valid rotation_signature

cases.description: Content signed with a revoked compromised key is untrusted even though it appears in history
cases.note: revoked keys declare compromise; clients MUST NOT accept signatures by the key after revocation and may conservatively reject all signatures when signing time is unavailable

cases.description: Content signed with a key not in current or history is untrusted
cases.note: Key is valid Ed25519 but not associated with this domain

cases.description: When domain is unreachable, Synapse witness provides second-class trust (not cached)
cases.note: Second-class trust is NOT cached to disk — must be re-verified each time
